prediction.summary: An insider threat incident involving user emma.hr has been detected. The user, who submitted her resignation 48 hours prior, accessed a large volume of sensitive HR and legal documents, including files outside her normal role scope. She then performed a bulk download of 842MB of data, copied it to a removable USB device, and initiated a sync to a personal cloud storage service, indicating a deliberate attempt to exfiltrate confidential company data.

timeline.event: User emma.hr accessed /legal/merger_draft.docx (outside normal role scope)
User emma.hr performed bulk download of 842MB archive /tmp/eh_export.zip
User emma.hr copied 842MB to removable device E:\backup
User emma.hr connected personal cloud sync client sync.evil-drop.io
DLP policy violation: HR confidential data staged for external sync by emma.hr
SIEM detected insider threat pattern: bulk sensitive file access + USB + cloud sync
SIEM noted user emma.hr resignation submitted 48 hours ago, risk score elevated
SIEM identified MITRE T1530 Data from Cloud Storage / internal collection
User emma.hr accessed 47 files outside normal role scope in 54 minutes

recommendations: Immediately disable all accounts and revoke access for user emma.hr to prevent further data exfiltration.
Initiate a forensic investigation of LAPTOP-HR-09 and any other devices used by emma.hr to identify the full scope of exfiltrated data.
Investigate the 'sync.evil-drop.io' cloud service to confirm data presence and explore options for data recovery or removal.
Review emma.hr's access logs for the past several weeks/months for any other suspicious activity or data access patterns.
Assess the impact of the exfiltrated data (e.g., PII, intellectual property) and engage legal/compliance teams as necessary.
